existing_Entity_Description: Pemberton is a municipality in Sea-to-Sky, British Columbia, Canada, within the Squamish-Lillooet. As of the 2021 Canadian Census, Pemberton had a population of 3,100. The community is situated in the world-famous Sea-to-Sky corridor from Vancouver to Whistler and beyond. an international tourism destination with premium pricing potential. The region features coastal-mountain climate with legendary snowfalls at elevation.

REGION_BUNDLE.climate: Steep climate gradient in 120 km: mild wet coastal at Squamish to alpine snow country at Whistler; heavy winter snow loads and freeze-thaw at elevation; intense short building seasons up-corridor.
REGION_BUNDLE.terrain: The Sea-to-Sky Highway (Highway 99) corridor from Howe Sound fjord through the Coast Mountains: granite walls (the Stawamus Chief), glacier-fed rivers, and alpine terrain around Whistler and Pemberton.
REGION_BUNDLE.economy: Whistler Blackcomb (North America's largest ski resort) drives a four-season tourism economy; Squamish is one of BC's fastest-growing municipalities with an outdoor-recreation and commuter economy; Pemberton anchors farming (famous seed potatoes).
REGION_BUNDLE.building_stock: Alpine chalets and lodges built for snow load, resort strata condos in Whistler, and new family subdivisions in Squamish; Pemberton mixes farm properties and new development.
